description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped stone with a roughly triangular profile and a coarse, granular texture. Its surface displays a mix of earthy brown and gray tones, with some areas appearing slightly smoother and others more rugged. The stone has a visibly fractured edge, suggesting it was broken from a larger rock, and there are subtle speckles and mineral inclusions throughout. One side shows a flatter, lighter gray face, while the other sides are more rounded and mottled. There are faint markings or writing visible on one surface, possibly for identification or cataloging. The overall appearance suggests a naturally weathered stone, suitable for use as an urban obstacle or barrier.
description: A medium, irregularly shaped stone with a coarse, mottled brown and gray surface, featuring fractured edges and mineral speckles.
description_view_0: Shows a rounded, brownish surface with visible granular texture and faint markings near the top.
description_view_1: Displays a darker, more angular side with a pronounced edge and rougher texture.
description_view_2: Highlights a flatter, lighter gray face with fine speckling and a smoother appearance.
description_view_3: Reveals a broad, brownish surface with a mix of rough and slightly smoother areas, and visible mineral inclusions.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or obstacle on pavement
attribute_pattern: Subtle mottling and speckling with no regular pattern; some faint mineral inclusions and possible catalog markings.
attribute_weathering: Rough and naturally weathered with minor surface smoothing and visible fractured edges.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with smooth man-made surfaces but may blend with other natural stones or landscaping elements.
